questions.question: Justify that the maximum number of zeroes of a polynomial is equal to its degree.
questions.answer: A polynomial of degree n can have at most n zeroes because its graph can intersect the x-axis at most n times.

questions.question: Explain why the graph of a quadratic polynomial cannot intersect the x-axis more than twice.
questions.answer: The graph of a quadratic polynomial is a parabola, which can intersect the x-axis at a maximum of two points.

questions.question: Explain why polynomials are considered continuous functions.
questions.answer: Polynomials have no breaks, jumps or holes in their graphs, making them continuous functions.

questions.question: Explain why the zero polynomial does not fit into the degree-zero classification.
questions.answer: The zero polynomial has no non-zero term, so its degree cannot be defined.

questions.question: Explain how changing coefficients affects the graph of a polynomial.
questions.answer: Changing coefficients alters the steepness, direction and position of the polynomial graph.

questions.question: Explain why a polynomial cannot have infinitely many zeroes unless it is the zero polynomial.
questions.answer: Only the zero polynomial is zero for all values of the variable; other polynomials can have only finite zeroes.

questions.question: Explain why negative powers disqualify an expression from being a polynomial.
questions.answer: Negative powers violate the definition of a polynomial which requires non-negative integer exponents.

questions.question: Explain the importance of studying polynomials before quadratic equations.
questions.answer: Understanding polynomials builds the foundation for quadratic equations, graphs and higher algebra.

questions.question: Explain why polynomial graphs are smooth and predictable.
questions.answer: Polynomial graphs are smooth because they do not involve division by variables or radicals.

questions.question: Explain why polynomials are preferred in curve fitting.
questions.answer: Polynomials are flexible and easy to compute, making them suitable for approximating data trends.

questions.question: Explain how the degree of a polynomial limits its number of turning points.
questions.answer: A polynomial of degree n can have at most n−1 turning points.

questions.question: Explain why p(0) gives the constant term of a polynomial.
questions.answer: Substituting x = 0 eliminates all variable terms, leaving only the constant term.

questions.question: Explain the mathematical significance of zeroes of polynomials.
questions.answer: Zeroes help solve equations, analyze graphs and understand polynomial behavior.

questions.question: Explain why the leading term dominates the behavior of a polynomial for large values of x.
questions.answer: For large values of x, the highest power term grows fastest and dominates the polynomial’s value.

questions.question: Explain why polynomial functions are closed under addition and multiplication.
questions.answer: Adding or multiplying polynomials results in expressions that still satisfy polynomial conditions.

questions.question: Explain why polynomial division may result in a remainder.
questions.answer: Because the divisor may not divide the dividend exactly, leaving a remainder of lower degree.

questions.question: Explain the importance of degree in determining the nature of polynomial graphs.
questions.answer: The degree determines the number of zeroes, turning points and overall shape of the graph.

questions.question: Explain why polynomials are widely used in physics and engineering.
questions.answer: Polynomials model motion, forces and relationships with smooth and predictable behavior.

questions.question: Explain why a polynomial of odd degree must have at least one real zero.
questions.answer: Its graph extends in opposite directions at the ends, ensuring at least one x-axis crossing.

questions.question: Explain how polynomial concepts prepare students for higher algebra.
questions.answer: They introduce functions, graphs, equations and modelling used in advanced mathematics.

questions.question: Explain why rational expressions are excluded from polynomial classification.
questions.answer: Because rational expressions may have variables in denominators or negative powers.

questions.question: Explain how polynomial graphs help in predicting behavior of functions.
questions.answer: Graphs reveal trends, intercepts and end behavior of polynomial functions.

questions.question: Explain why the zero polynomial is unique among all polynomials.
questions.answer: It is the only polynomial that is zero for all values of the variable.

questions.question: Explain the limitations of graphical methods in finding zeroes of polynomials.
questions.answer: Graphical methods give approximate values and depend on scale and accuracy of drawing.

questions.question: Explain the overall importance of polynomials in mathematics and science.
questions.answer: Polynomials form the backbone of algebra, modelling, calculus and scientific computation.
